steps.title: Write the budget constraint
steps.content:
Fixed cost is 30 dollars, and variable cost is 10.25 dollars per attendee. For $n$ attendees, total cost must not exceed 250 dollars:
$$30 + 10.25n \leq 250$$

steps.title: Solve for n and determine the answer
steps.content:
Subtract 30 from both sides:
$$10.25n \leq 220$$
Divide both sides by 10.25:
$$n \leq \frac{220}{10.25} = \frac{880}{41} \approx 21.46$$
Since the number of attendees must be a whole number, round down to 21. With 21 attendees, the cost is $30 + 10.25(21) = 245.25$, which stays within the 250 dollar budget. The answer is $21$.
